title: pull_request_target with checkout of PR head SHA — untrusted PR code runs with repo secrets and write permissions
exploit: Attacker submits a PR modifying ci.yml or a script invoked by it; pull_request_target runs the attacker's code with full repo write permissions and access to all secrets — classic GitHub Actions poisoned pipeline execution (PPE)
fix: Use pull_request (not pull_request_target) for untrusted PRs; if pull_request_target is required, never check out the PR head — check out base branch only
evidence:
pull_request_target:
    branches: [main]
  ...
  - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    with:
      ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}

title: Secret value echoed into CI log — visible to anyone with workflow log access
exploit: GitHub masks ${{ secrets.X }} in log output but the echo exposes the raw value; any user with Actions log read access (including forked PR authors on some org settings) sees the key
fix: Remove the debug echo; use runner:debug for secret-safe debugging or never log secret values

title: Actions not SHA-pinned — tag references can be silently moved to malicious commits
exploit: Action maintainer (or an attacker who compromises their account) moves the @v4 tag to a commit that exfiltrates secrets or injects code into the build; pinning to a SHA prevents this
fix: Pin every action to a full commit SHA: uses: actions/checkout@<40-char-sha>; use a tool like Dependabot or pin-github-action to maintain pins

title: No permissions block in deploy workflow — job has implicit write-all permissions on repo contents
exploit: Without an explicit permissions block, GitHub grants the job write access to contents, packages, deployments, etc.; a supply-chain compromise in any step can push to the repo or overwrite releases
fix: Add a top-level permissions: block with minimum required scopes (e.g., contents: read) and override per-job only when needed
evidence:
name: Deploy
# VULNERABILITY: no permissions block — job has implicit write-all permissions on repo contents
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

title: curl | bash fetches and executes an unpinned remote script at deploy time — supply chain code execution
exploit: The remote script at main branch can be changed at any time; if the repo is compromised the deploy pipeline executes the attacker's code with access to all secrets and deployment credentials
fix: Download the script, verify its checksum against a known hash, then execute; or vendor the tool in the repo instead
